meta.professor_methodology: Treat y as function of x, use chain rule
meta.description: Finding derivatives when y is not explicitly defined as function of x

patterns.name: Implicit Differentiation
patterns.recognition_signal: y not isolated, equation involving both x and y
canonical_forms: x^2 + y^2 = r^2, \quad xy + \sin y = x
patterns.logic: Differentiate both sides, apply chain rule to y terms, solve for dy/dx

professor_tips: Every y term gets a dy/dx factor
Product rule applies when x and y are multiplied
Final answer can have both x and y

confidence_checklist: I remember to use chain rule on y terms
I can find tangent lines to implicit curves
